column2: Preface The implementation of the Constitution of Kenya created increased demand for comprehensive and disaggregated statistical information to inform evidence-based policy formulation, planning, budgeting process, monitoring and evaluation of various programmes at the county level. The 2020 Makueni County Statistical Abstract (CSA) is the second of its kind to be developed by Makueni County and will subsequently be produced annually. The Statistical Abstract will be a single source of information covering various sectors of the economy at the county level for the period 2015-2019. The CSA substantially derived the data used from the administrative sources generated by Government agencies in the county. In addition, some statistics were compiled from various survey reports and 2019 Kenya Population and Housing Census. The information published in this 2020 CSA will address data gaps at the county level and strengthen evidence-based decision making. The CSA is expected to provide a reliable source of information for planning, policy formulation, monitoring and evaluation processes. Therefore, the data contained in this CSA and previous CSA will also enable data users to gauge the performance of the county. Additionally, in implementing and tracking the UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), Africa Agenda 2063, Kenya Vision 2030, Makueni Vision 2025, data will be instrumental. It is in this breadth that we have partnered with Kenya National Bureau of Statistics (KNBS) to strengthen the county statistics function. ICT plays a pivotal role in data management and statistics. The Covid-19 pandemic has demonstrated indeed, data and ICT are key ingredients for development. As a county we are committed to ensure integration of ICT in data and statistics processes. The county has a comprehensive and elaborate public participation framework from the village level. We are leveraging on this platform to champion for citizen generated data. In this regard, we are welcome to work with all stakeholders to support citizen generated data in order to improve on the quality of statistical information, enhance dissemination and accessibility of statistics at the county level. H.E. Prof Kivutha Kibwana Governor, Makueni County CCoouunnttyy SSttaattiissttiiccaall AAbbssttrraacctt,, 22002200 xxii
